Title: Haplotype-resolved genome assembly provides new insights into the genomic origin of purple colour in Prunus mume

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation contributing to purple coloration; antioxidant roles in plant organs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activated downstream of PmmMYB10.5b and associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmANS was highly expressed in purple tissues and differentially expressed between green and purple hybrid individuals. 2. Its promoter was directly activated by PmmMYB10.5b in dual-luciferase assays. 3. PmmMYB10.5b bound MYB motifs in ABG promoters and promoted late-pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; RT-qPCR in transgenic hairy roots; dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves and fruits; elevated in purple tissues and in PmmMYB10.5b-overexpressing hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native allelic PmmANS promoters in reporter assays; CaMV 35S for effector overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf and fruit developmental stages; comparison of purple and green individuals
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied primarily as a direct downstream anthocyanin biosynthetic gene target rather than as the main causal locus.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a downstream readout/stacking target with PmmMYB10.5b for anthocyanin enhancement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates anthocyanidins to generate stable anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation contributing to purple coloration; antioxidant roles.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by PmmMYB10.5b and facilitates stable anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmUF3GT was among the downstream genes especially promoted by PmmMYB10.5b. 2. Its promoter showed strong activation in dual-luciferase assays with PmmMYB10.5b. 3. EMSA confirmed direct binding of PmmMYB10.5b to MYB motifs in the PmmUF3GT promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; RT-qPCR in transgenic hairy roots; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in leaves and fruits; elevated in purple tissues and upon PmmMYB10.5b overexpression.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native allelic PmmUF3GT promoters in reporter assays; CaMV 35S for effector overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf and fruit developmental stages; purple versus green tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of the most important late biosynthetic genes causing conversion to stable anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for enhancing visible anthocyanin deposition in combination with upstream regulators.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to produce dihydroflavonols feeding anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter is activated by PmmMYB10.5b, supporting increased flux toward anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmF3OH promoter responded to PmmMYB10.5b in dual-luciferase assays. 2. It functions as a direct anthocyanin biosynthetic gene target contributing precursor supply. 3. Activation is part of the broader transcriptional induction of ABGs in purple tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in colored leaves/fruits as part of ABG program.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native allelic PmmF3OH promoters in reporter assays; CaMV 35S for effector overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental color change in leaves and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Written as PmmF30H in parts of the text, clearly referring to F3OH/F3H-type anthocyanin biosynthetic enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be co-engineered with regulator PmmMYB10.5b to reinforce precursor flux.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB; S6 clade anthocyanin regulator; contains M1 motif
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Prunus mume 'Meiren' haplotype C allele inherited from P. cerasifera 'Pissardii'
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Meiren / inherited from Pissardii-derived HC allele
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core activator and major causal regulator underlying purple colour; expression activated by inversion-altered promoter and showing consistent allele-specific expression.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, stems, flowers, fruits, callus, hairy roots, seed coat/seedlings/leaves/inflorescence in heterologous Arabidopsis assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Purple/red pigmentation and antioxidant protection in plant organs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and causes purple coloration in Meiren organs.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through direct promoter binding; co-activation with bHLH partner via protein complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, especially late genes including PmmANS and PmmUF3GT; also induces PmmbHLH3 expression in overexpression lines.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PmmUF3GT alleles; PmmF3OH/F30H alleles; PmmANS alleles; broader anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: 1.8 Mb inversion (INV1023) in haplotype C alters promoter and activates expression; developmental stage; possibly light/temperature context discussed but not directly established for this gene
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing leaves and fruits of purple Meiren; consistent HC-biased allele-specific expression across stages
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in purple individuals and Meiren, low/absent in green individuals and green-leaved P. cerasifera
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms PmmMYB10.5b-PmmbHLH3 activation complex; also self-interacts
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL/co-segregation with inversion; allele-specific expression analysis; RT-qPCR; transient overexpression in tobacco; stable overexpression in Arabidopsis; hairy-root overexpression in P. mume; Y2H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf and fruit developmental stages; purple versus green segregants; transient assays in tobacco; Arabidopsis overexpression; hairy-root transformation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression; native PmmMYB10.5b promoter assayed by GUS and LUC
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: HiFi genome assembly; Hi-C; RNA-seq; Iso-seq; resequencing; BioProject PRJNA1201869
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Leaf colour instability and environmental responsiveness are discussed for Meiren, but no direct growth penalty from PmmMYB10.5b reported.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmMYB10.5b is located at the distal breakpoint of the 1.8 Mb inversion that completely co-segregates with purple colour. 2. The inversion changes the promoter ~137 bp upstream of ATG, greatly increasing promoter activity and activating expression. 3. PmmMYB10.5b shows consistent HC-biased allele-specific expression across leaf and fruit development and in purple segregants. 4. Overexpression induces anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco, Arabidopsis, and P. mume hairy roots. 5. PmmMYB10.5b directly binds promoters of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and activates them, especially PmmANS and PmmUF3GT. 6. It physically interacts with PmmbHLH3 as a co-activator complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent marker/engineering target for purple coloration in Prunus; inversion and/or activated allele can be used for marker-assisted selection, introgression, or transgenic editing strategies.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal activation is attributed to promoter alteration by inversion inherited from Pissardii rather than from green-leaved P. cerasifera. Both coding alleles are functionally competent; expression difference is the key determinant.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH clade B flavonoid regulator; homologous to AtTT8
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key co-activator partner required for strong anthocyanin induction by PmmMYB10.5 alleles.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with PmmMYB10.5b to promote anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes by partnering with PmmMYB10.5b
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via complex with PmmMYB10.5b
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Expression correlated with purple tissues; induced in PmmMYB10.5b overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar expression pattern to PmmMYB10.5b; differentially expressed in Meiren and purple-leaf individuals
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves and fruits, especially purple tissues
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with PmmMYB10.5b; also interacts with repressors PmmMYBR1 and PmmMYBR2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling; transient co-infiltration in tobacco; Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Purple versus green individuals; transient tobacco assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmbHLH3 alleles are homologous to AtTT8-type anthocyanin regulators. 2. Co-expression with PmmMYB10.5 alleles in tobacco triggers strong anthocyanin accumulation. 3. PmmbHLH3 physically interacts with PmmMYB10.5b and is part of the activation complex. 4. It also interacts with MYB repressors, suggesting competitive regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering partner with PmmMYB10.5b to maximize anthocyanin production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important mechanistically, it is presented as partner/cofactor rather than the primary causal locus for the inversion-associated purple trait.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB repressor with C1 and C2 motifs; S4 clade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator that counteracts the PmmMYB10.5b activation module.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases anthocyanin accumulation when co-expressed with PmmMYB10.5b and PmmbHLH3.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression/competitive inhibition through interaction with bHLH partner and MYB activator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes indirectly via competition in regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Expression pattern similar to PmmMYB10.5b and ABGs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to PmmMYB10.5b during developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; transient overexpression in tobacco; dual-luciferase assay; Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May attenuate pigment accumulation; no broader tradeoff reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmMYBR1 encodes a nuclear R2R3-MYB repressor containing C1/C2 motifs. 2. Co-expression reduces anthocyanin accumulation in tobacco. 3. Dual-luciferase assays confirm inhibitory activity. 4. It interacts with PmmbHLH3 and PmmMYB10.5b, likely competing with the activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for knockout/editing to enhance anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was functionally tested in Results as a core antagonist of the causal activator module.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB repressor with C1 and C2 motifs; S4 clade
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator antagonizing activator complex function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases anthocyanin accumulation when co-expressed with activator components.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Competitive repression via interaction with PmmbHLH3
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes indirectly via regulatory complex competition
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Expression pattern similar to PmmMYB10.5b and ABGs
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to PmmMYB10.5b during developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; transient overexpression in tobacco; dual-luciferase assay; Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce pigment accumulation; no additional tradeoff reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmmMYBR2 is a nuclear R2R3-MYB repressor with C1/C2 motifs. 2. It suppresses anthocyanin accumulation in transient assays. 3. Dual-luciferase assays support inhibitory activity. 4. It interacts with PmmbHLH3 and likely competes against the PmmMYB10.5b activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate repressor target for editing to increase purple pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally validated as a repressor in the same regulatory module.
